text.title: Supply Chain Risk Checklist Spike Examples
text.summary: End-to-end worked example illustrating the methodology against a realistic input.

example.title: xz-utils evaluated with the 7 signals (retrospective)
example.note: library=xz; ecosystem=apt/generic. signals: license=0 (BSD), maintainer_count=3 (1 active), last_release_age=2 (slow), cve_history_12mo=3 (CVE-2024-3094 critical), stars_to_downloads=1, transitive_depth=0, vendor_lock_in=0. aggregate=9. verdict=yellow. remediation=pin-and-monitor (pre-incident); replace (post-incident). decay_date set to 30d. Lesson: maintainer_count=3 + cve_history=3 alone would have triggered yellow even without disclosure.

example.title: Stars-only adoption
example.note: Team adopted 'awesome-utils' with 14k stars; signals: license=3 (custom), maintainer_count=3 (solo), last_release_age=3 (>1y), cve=0 (no disclosures yet), stars_to_downloads=2 (high), transitive_depth=2, vendor_lock_in=1. aggregate=14 (red). Tree caught: stars-only-eval + custom-license-adopted → replace.
